title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Cross-Agent Certification Board
summary: Fresh writing, coding, tool, security, and long-task cases requiring prompt design, diagnosis, cross-provider adaptation, and evaluation evidence. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.
Allocate context to governing instructions, task evidence, examples, and tool results according to value.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Task Contract evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Task Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Task Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Prompt quality can be judged without a defined task outcome. This fails the Task Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: OpenAI Codex Manual: Prompting and Best Practices (Verified current 2026-07-13) — Controls Codex prompting, Plan mode, AGENTS.md, verification, and surface guidance.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.

hints: Name the Task Contract precondition or invariant that the exam case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed exam case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Prompt quality can be judged without a defined task outcome.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise
